programme.desc: James must move in with his girlfriend of three months after being evicted from his place, and soon he discovers that he doesn't want to be with her anymore. His hope is to get her to break up with him but still let him sleep on the couch.

programme.title: Gun
programme.desc: Rich is the police's prime target against the war on gun runners. When an exchange goes bad, paranoia ensues, causing rifts among Rich and his team.

programme.title: Born Again Baddie

After expanding her business and becoming the queen pin of the city, tragedy strikes and it forces her to find solace and reflection on her past life choices.

programme.title: Game of Cards
programme.desc: When data analyst Nicole Tucker’s car is broken into and a mysterious military-issued card tied to her family legacy is stolen, she’s thrust into a dangerous game of betrayal. Her search for the truth uncovers a chilling connection between the theft and her boyfriend Michael, forcing Nicole to confront dark secrets, deadly allies, and a past that refuses to stay buried.

programme.title: Pretty Girls Kill
programme.desc: In 2004, Ruby, a college student witnesses a supernatural horror at a local sorority house where three women, known as The Takers, kill and drain the life out of her friends to preserve their youth and beauty. 20 years later, now a detective, ghosts from her past resurface, and she must face these demons once again.

programme.title: Rhythm and Revenge
programme.desc: After losing her parents, Journi, a talented but troubled teenager, moves in with her aunt and cousin for a fresh start. As she navigates the pressures of competitive dance, strange accidents begin to occur around her. Suspicion grows, but Journi always seems one step ahead, charming her way out of trouble. However, when the truth starts to unravel, danger lurks closer than anyone could have imagined.

When the remains of an apartheid-era policeman are discovered 60 years after he went missing, a retired singer revisits her past to help with the investigation. But how much does she know, and what is she holding back?

programme.title: Transmorphers
programme.desc: Alien robots force humanity underground. After 300 years, a group of humans plan to defeat the machines.

programme.title: Triassic World
programme.desc: Dinosaurs used to grow human organs for transplants escape. The scientists that created them must fight back.

programme.title: Troy: The Odyssey
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Trojan War, Odysseus must battle gods and monsters as he journeys home.

programme.title: Universal Soldiers
programme.desc: Unstable and unstoppable genetically modified super soldiers escape from their maximum security holding cells.

programme.title: San Andreas Quake
programme.desc: A discredited seismologist warns of an impending 12.7 earthquake and races to get her family to safety.

programme.title: The Prey (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Three young couples set off into the mountains for a weekend of climbing, drinking and lovemaking. But little do they know that they are stumbling into the terrain of a fearsome predator - a wild man, horrifically burned as a child many years ago in a fire which engulfed his gypsy camp and left only him alive. Now he roams the woods in search of his next human prey.

programme.title: Mermaid Down
programme.desc: A mermaid is ripped from the Pacific, her tail is chopped off and she's thrown into a mental home where no one believes she is a mermaid.

programme.title: The Haunting of Molly Hartley
programme.desc: Molly Hartley hopes that with her mother locked away in a mental ward, she canforget the violence of her familys past and start over. She has found a new life, a new school and a new boyfriend. But when her secret past and haunting nightmares continue, Molly fears she has inherited her mothers psychosis. What she doesnt know is that shes inherited something else a secret far more horrifying than any fate she can imagine and a destiny she cannot avoid.

programme.title: The House By the Cemetery
programme.desc: A young family moves from their cramped New York City apartment to a spacious new home in New England. But this is no ordinary house in the country: the previous owner was the deranged Dr. Freudstein, whose monstrous human experiments have left a legacy of bloody mayhem. Now, someone - or something - is alive in the basement, and home sweet home is about to become a horrific hell on earth.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Rescues Pearl" - Since Mr. Brewster doesn't want to get married, but since the whole county knows that Cousin Pearl had her hat set for him, Jed comes up with a plan for her to save face by having Mr. Brewster proposed in public and then have her turn him down. Jed didn't count on Mr. Brewster's background in the theater and him making a huge production out of it.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Buys Stock" - Grannie prepares her special mash to help cure Mrs. Drysdale of her drinking problem while Jed does as Mr. Drysdale suggests and buys stock: cows, goats, pigs, and chickens! Meanwhile, Mrs. Drysdale recovers from the shock of her first meeting with the Clampetts and is convinced she's imagining things.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Girl From Home" - A father and daughter from back home come to Beverly Hills to meet Jed.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Giant Jackrabbit" - Granny encounters a kangaroo, and thinks she's found a giant jackrabbit, but no one will believe her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro Goes to School" - Jed enrolls Jethro at an exclusive Beverly Hills elementary school. Millicent Schuyler-Potts, the proprietor of the school, is aghast when Jed and Jethro show up, convinced this is part of some ghastly hoax. After Jed says that banker Milburn Drysdale is his neighbor, she calls the banker. It turns out that Drysdale's bank holds the mortgage on the school. Schulyer-Potts suddenly warms to the charms of the Clampetts, even if it means accepting Jethro as a fifth grader.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Race for Queen" - Elly May competes in a contest to determine "The Queen of Beverly Hills."

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Lafe Lingers On" - Lafe Crick comes back in another scheme to get rich off the Clampetts' wealth.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Mr. Drysdale's idea for Christmas presents are ones only fit for coastal California, like diving suits and a boat, and a television set, which Granny thinks is some kind of fancy washing machine.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Needs a Maw" - Mr. Drysdale needs investors for his real estate development project, so he enlists the Widow Fenwick, and aims to cement the deal by hitching her to Jed Clampett, who is looking to find a mother for his daughter, Elly May.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Chickadee Returns" - Jethro thinks he's in love with Chickadee Laverne, a stripper he met at the bank. She and the Clampetts have meet and have different ideas about what an "engagement" is, while Miss Hathaway tries to avert this disaster.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro's First Love" - Jethro decides that he wants to go a-courtin', so after Uncle Jed gives him a little education, he finds himself a woman--who happens to be a stripper. As usual, neither she or the Clampetts actually understand what the other is really saying.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Starts to School" - Mrs. Drysdale recommends Elly for a high-brow finishing school in hopes that humiliating her will drive the Clampetts away; but instead Miss Hathaway convinces the girls at the school that Elly is a fashion trend-setter. Elly invites the filthy rich Fenwicks over, thinking they're destitute.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: An old friend of Lucas's returns to town in the company of a shady gambler and is accused of cheating at cards.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Several men are vying for a prosperous rancher's daughter's affections. When she is found dead, one of them becomes the prime suspect in her murder.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas receives a series of mysterious threatening notes from an ex-convict with a grudge.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: After shooting a man in self-defense, Andy Moon turns to Lucas for protection from the man's brothers.

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.title: We're putting a V10 into a Mazda MX-5! | Ep.3
programme.desc: A couple of years ago, Mike Fernie set out to make a truly unique MX-5 project car, one powered by a V10 engine. But not just any V10 engine, a tiny, 2.0-litre powertrain from the 1990s. Since then, it has been a turbulent development process to say the least, so Mike has decided to film an update and keep you guys across what is happening.

programme.title: The 7.7-litre V12 supercar that was too scary for anyone to drive
programme.desc: Does it get any better than a 7.7-litre V12 British supercar, with GT1 racecar engineering and 840bhp? Mike delves into the depths of the only road legal TVR Cerbera Speed 12 ever built by the TVR factory, in all of its carbon fibre glory.

programme.title: First drive of our manual-converted 420bhp Jaguar XKR!
programme.desc: Our manual Jaguar XKR is out on the roads, and Mike Fernie has a burning question about the car – Have we made a better Jag?

programme.title: Turning Richard Hammond's Grand Tour Subaru Impreza into a 500bhp monster! | Project Martin Ep.5
programme.desc: After getting him back road-worthy, Richard Hammond has tasked Mike Fernie with building an engine to take Martin the Subaru's performance to another level. Mike has brought the Grand Tour Impreza to Subaru experts Pole Position UK to turn the car into a monster!

programme.title: Fixing James May’s CRASHED Tesla Model S at The Smallest Cog
programme.desc: James May threw Mike Fernie the keys to his daily driver, a Tesla Model S, to be repaired at The Smallest Cog. Andrew Greenhouse and Mike get stuck in to remedy all of the EV's issues, before meeting up with James and reuniting him with the car.

programme.title: Restoring Richard Hammond's Subaru from The Grand Tour | Project Martin Ep.3
programme.desc: The journey to repair Richard Hammond's Subaru Impreza continues!

programme.title: Building Richard Hammond's Grand Tour Subaru into a 500hp monster | Project Martin Ep.6
programme.desc: After stripping Martin the Impreza's engine down to bare bones, Mike and the guys from Pole Position set about building the flat-four engine back up, but stronger and more powerful than ever. Aiming for 500hp, Martin gains some upgraded bits that should see him become an absolute weapon.

programme.title: Richard Hammond hosted his first car meet! | Project Jaaaag Ep.9 – FINALE!
programme.desc: After many months of restoration and modification, Project Jaaaag comes to a crescendo! Richard and Mike have invited a bunch of Jaguar enthusiasts down to The Smallest Cog to judge the two DriveTribe Jags – Richard's XJR and Mike's manual XKR. Who will take the crown of the coolest Jag on the channel?!

programme.title: We’ve bought a cheap (and ridiculously rare) forgotten ’90s performance car!
programme.desc: It's new car time! Mike Fernie and Ben Collins have treated themselves to new motors, and in this video Mike reveals his 1990s performance saloon that most people will have forgotten about.

programme.title: £4000 MANUAL XKR Inspected By Jaguar!
programme.desc: After manual-swapping our Japanese imported Jaguar XKR, Mike takes the car back home to Jaguar HQ to have it inspected by the experts at the Jaguar Daimler Heritage Trust. Does it pass the test?

programme.title: Old 911 vs new 911: Can an amateur driver beat ex-Stig Ben Collins?
programme.desc: Around a track, of course a brand new 911 992 Turbo S is going to beat a 997 Turbo. But what if you put an amateur driver in the 992, and the Stig in the 997? We pitched GoPro CEO Nick Woodman up against Ben Collins to see if it’s possible to take down the Stig with newer machinery.

programme.title: James May roasts Jeremy Clarkson & Richard Hammond's cars
programme.desc: James May is back for another roast! And this time he's going after Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond's car collections.

programme.title: Why Isn’t There A 7-Cylinder Engine?
programme.desc: We have single cylinder engines, twins, triples, four-pots, inline fives, straight-sixes, but then we start to skip some steps. We jump to V8s, V10s and V12s, even V- or W16s. It’s a question many of you probably have never even thought to ask, but now that you’ve clicked on this video, let’s talk about it – why is there no such thing as a 7-cylinder engine?

programme.title: We're putting a V10 in a Mazda MX-5! | Ep.6
programme.desc: We're back at it with the Mk1 V10 MX-5, and in this episode we're cutting the Mazda to see just how low we can get the engine. It's time for that S85 to nestle right in there!

programme.title: Should this be Izzy Hammond's new car?
programme.desc: Izzy Hammond needs a new car, and while Richard was away filming, he set Mike Fernie the goal of finding a new potential daily car for her. He may have gone slightly over-budget, but he reckons he's found the perfect car for Izzy's new London life.

programme.title: What does the old Stig actually drive? – NEW CAR!
programme.desc: Last year, we revealed that ex-Stig Ben Collins drives a 997 911 Turbo as a daily driver. But in 2023, he has added a car to the Stig garage, and this one is an absolute peach.

programme.title: We're putting a V10 into a Mazda MX-5! | Ep.4
programme.desc: The V10 MX-5 project takes a massive leap forward, both with news on the tricky Connaught crankshaft, but also a completely fresh sheet of paper – ANOTHER V10 MX-5!

programme.title: Is our 0.6-litre Suzuki Cappuccino faster than a Mk1 MX-5? | TRACK BATTLE Ft. Abbie Eaton
programme.desc: We bought a tiny little kei car – a Suzuki Cappuccino – earlier this year with one goal in mind; making it quicker than the current go-to entry-level sportscar, the Mazda MX-5. Has Mike accomplished his goal? With the help of Abbie Eaton, let's find out!

programme.title: Richard Hammond drives his 530bhp Grand Tour Subaru for the first time – And it's incredible!
programme.desc: Richard Hammond has been waiting a long time to test out his newly-tuned Subaru Impreza from the Grand Tour. After 2023 was spent building Martin the Subaru into a 530bhp, all-wheel drive, manual monster, Richard is finally thrown the keys to take it for a spin on his favourite Welsh Highland road.

programme.title: We're putting a V10 into a Mazda MX-5! | Ep.5
programme.desc: We are making two V10 Mazda MX-5s! While we wait for the crazy Connaught 2.0-litre engine to be finished, we are cracking on building a car with an existing V10. And here it is! Let the games begin.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 4 (ME4)-PSPC champion Ramon Colillas surges into the top five as just 40 players remain in the EPT Monte Carlo Main Event. Day 3 intensifies as competitors battle to reach the final two tables. With the finish line in sight, who has what it takes to keep their championship dreams alive?

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 5 (ME5)-The field narrows to just 16 survivors, including EPT Dublin champion Niall Farrell and PSPC winner Ramon Colillas. With over €41,000 already locked up, the stakes couldn't be higher as players fight for their place at the final table of nine. Every hand could be the difference between glory and heartbreak.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 6 (ME6)-Nine players remain, but only six will advance to the final day with a shot at nearly €1 million and poker immortality. Can PSPC champion Ramon Colillas extend his remarkable run to another major final table, or will his epic journey end just short of the ultimate prize?

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 7 (ME7)-The moment of truth arrives at the EPT Monte Carlo final table. Six players-two businessmen, one qualifier, and three seasoned professionals-wage the ultimate battle for almost €1 million and the coveted EPT trophy. After days of intense competition, only one can claim poker's most prestigious prize.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: With a New Orleans–inspired menu now set for his friend Bryan Ford, Dave starts testing out this week’s recipes: barbecue turkey legs, meat pies, coconut flatbread, and coconut beignets.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: "Preparing for Bryan Ford" - This week's guest, Bryan Ford, is on his way to the studio. With a coconut-heavy menu now locked in, Dave starts making his three-breads-in-one dough, and gets the turkey necks cooking.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: "Dinner with Bryan Ford" - Our guest this week is Bryan Ford, for whom Dave and Chris Ying have prepared a menu that’s heavy on coconut, devoid of shellfish (Bryan’s allergic), and heavily inspired by New Orleans.

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Korean Tangerine Salad with Chile: Deuki Hong and Matt Rodbard stopped by the studio to share some of their fantastic recipes from Koreaworld: A Cookbook! Check out this Jeju Tangerine Chile Salad...a crisp, refreshing, tangy dish with some (adjustable) heat.

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Korean "Drug Eggs" Extremely Addicting Soy Sauce-Marinated Eggs with Rice (Mayak Gyeranbap): Deuki Hong and Matt Rodbard, authors of Koreaworld: A Cookbook, make Mayak Gyeranbap! These sweet, garlicky, soy-infused eggs can be loosely translated as "drug eggs," so named because of their magnetic craveability, a quality they share with their spiritual brethren, Taiwanese tea eggs. The eggs are cooked to a soft and yolky consistency and served over rice as a full meal or by themselves for a robust snack.

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Whole Fried Smashed Rockfish: Deuki Hong and Matt Rodbard, authors of Koreaworld: A Cookbook, make a dish they first experienced on the eastern coast of Jeju Island—a whole fried rockfish ladled with a sticky, fiery sauce and then smashed into bite-size pieces. A truly brilliant way to do whole fish!

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "David Chang's Black Friday Leftover Sandwich v2.0!" - Dave does another version of his work-in-progress leftover sandwich that he's been working on to present to the MVP of the Black Friday Jets vs Dolphins game.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Dave Arnold's Mom's Stuffing (your new go-to holiday recipe)" - We're excited to share this insane, soon-to-be-family-standard recipe from our good friend, Dave Arnold, who is admittedly is still trying to figure this out from his own mom's recipe. This is THE. ACTUAL. DAVE. ARNOLD. filming himself making the aforementioned famous stuffing with some of our flourishes of text and music. I mean...this is it. Nobody, including Dave Arnold, knows how long to cook it or at what temperature you should cook it at, but we're confident if you're still reading this, you're going to do just fine.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "A THANKSGIVING MIRACLE! David Chang and Friends Make a FULL Thanksgiving Dinner in 2 hours?!?!" - David Chang is joined by Chris Ying and Chris Bianco while they attempt to make an entire Thanksgiving dinner in less than 2 hours...no cuts, no tricks. Just people cooking, cussin', starting things on fire, and having a (mostly) nice time. Hey, it's the holidays...we're all miserable.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Barbecue Health Food: A little-known fact-Steven Raichlen’s first James Beard Award-winning book wasn’t about barbecue, but a book on healthy eating. This show harnesses the flavor- boosting, fat-melting power of live fire to produce grilled fare that not only tastes good but is good for you. From the paellas of Spain to the grilled beef salads of Southeast Asia.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Steak. Few other words in the English language have such power to make our hearts beat faster and our mouths water than sizzling slabs of beef seared over live fire. In this show we embark on some meaty globe hopping, exploring over-the-top steaks from France, Cambodia, and the American Southwest. Hot fire. Sizzling meat. It’s a carnivore’s dream come true.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Little Italy: When I was growing up, a trip to Baltimore’s Little Italy always meant culinary adventure. Today we celebrate Italian grilling and the food of Little Italys around North America, honoring the Italian reverence for simplicity and fresh seasonal ingredients, and the American passion for big flavors and ingenious grilling techniques.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: The Improbable Grill: In this show we explore three unexpected techniques for grilling. The first stands the vertical rotisserie used in Mexico and the Middle East. The second involves a piece of equipment you don’t usually find at a barbecue—a roofer’s torch. The third involves a mystery ingredient that virtually no one on the planet has ever grilled.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Cured and Smoked: In humankind’s long march to food security, two ancient preserving techniques have stood out over the millennia: curing and smoking. The first involves preserving foods with salt, soy sauce, or sodium nitrite, and the second technique involves blasting foods with flavorful clouds of wood smoke. Today, we’re pushing the envelope on traditional curing and smoking.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Maryland Crab Feast: This show focuses on my favorite food growing up: callinectes sapidus, better known as the Maryland blue crab. Maryland seafood markets and restaurants come alive with soft shell crabs, eaten shell and all, traditionally deep-fried, here seared over live fire. Which brings us to the summum of the Maryland crab experience: a crab feast known as Maryland steamed crabs.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Raichlen’s Rules: Desserts: Take your desserts to a new level of flavor with Grilled Pound Cake with Berry Salsa and Smoked Whipped Cream, Salt Slab-Grilled Pears with Amaretti And Smoked Whipped Cream, Dessert Quesadillas, Smoked Ice Cream with Rum Raisin Sauce.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Let Texans brag about brisket and Carolinians praise pulled pork shoulder. For the rest of us, the ultimate symbol of barbecue is ribs. In the American Midwest, ribs mean baby backs or other pork ribs, while in Korea, the bone of choice is the beef short rib. In the eastern Mediterranean, lamb ribs reign supreme. In this show we give you three of the best ribs ever.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Join Lidia as she makes a quick and easy Tomato and Ricotta Crostini. A Tuna Salad with Boiled Zucchini, and take comfort with Eggplant and Rice Parmigiana.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Lidia prepares a rich Meatball and Eggplant Tagliatelle, an easy Vegetable and Meat Casserole, and a rustic Lentil Country Salad.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Puebla, Mexico: According to Tony, the best line cooks in NYC come from Mexico, so he travels to the Puebla, and enjoys mole sauces, toasted ant eggs, fried worms, and the legendary pulque (a slightly hallucinogenic drink made from cactus sap).

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Tony travels to the grain-belt capital of Minneapolis, Minnesota to experience firsthand the conflict between creative independent operators and generic franchises.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: St. Petersburg, Russia: Tony continues his mission with a tour of local working-class specialties. He tries his hand at ice-fishing, drinks a lot of vodka, feasts on Russian foods, eats smoked fish and ends the outing with a dip in the outdoor cold pool.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Join Tony on his quest to characterize the perfect bar in New York City. Discover the importance of a maniacal bartender, the proper chemical makeup of drinks, the joys of seedy dive-bars, and the need for late-night snacks.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: San Sebastian, Spain: Tony explores the food-crazed Basque culture. Tony's guide, Chef Luis Irizar, takes Tony into the underworld of Basque gastronomic societies. Later, Tony explores the city's tapas bars.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Oaxaca, Mexico: Chef Tony Bourdain explores the exotic and colorful indigenous zone of Mexico's Oaxaca region where he tastes toasted grasshoppers and other delicacies. His adventure ends with mariachi music and, of course, lots of tequila.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: New York, NY: Passionate about his city, Tony gives us an intimate tour of the New York City foods he loves - sturgeon and bagels, fluke ceviche, pan-seared foie gras, braised veal cheek, authentic cheese and, of course, a hotdog.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: What is a Carioca? The natives of Rio de Janiero, Brazil are lovable scamps who find ways to avoid work, preferring the beach, flirting, and hanging out at restaurants and bars instead. Tony, under the tutelage of an exemplary Carioca, gives the lifestyle a try.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Cambodia and Japan: Taking his obsession with the film Apocalypse Now a little too seriously, Tony sets out for Pailin, reputed to be one of the most dangerous towns on earth. After his journey, Tony returns to Japan.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Portugal & Southwest France: Visiting his boss's family farm, Tony helps slaughter and feast on a pig the family has been fattening all year. Then, Tony heads to France to experience the rich delights of a foie gras farm.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: In New Orleans, Louisiana, Tony catches a Bayou alligator, cools off with a world-famous snow cone, gets in trouble with the law, eats jambalaya with a dancing chef, and relaxes to some local jazz.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Jumping into a Thai tuk tuk taxi, Tony attempts to soak up Thai culture, and get lost in the cuisine of Chiang Mai, Thailand.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Which style of BBQ reigns supreme - Kansas City, Houston, or North Carolina? Tony journeys to all three cities to taste for himself the secrets behind sauces, wood pits, and slow smokers.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Morocco (Sahara): Tony traverses the Moroccan desert with one goal in mind, to eat a mud-oven-roasted sheep.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Los Angeles, CA: New York chef, Anthony Bourdain, descends into the culinary underbelly of his metropolitan nemesis, Los Angeles, where he experiences the trials of celebrity fame and the joys of West Coast cuisine.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Napa, CA: Marooned in San Francisco, Tony tastes local shellfish and durian ice cream before embarking on a pilgrimage to experience the genius of Thomas Keller, chef and owner of The French Laundry.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: The Bumble Nums - Super Simple Collection 1
programme.desc: Once Humble, Grumble, and Stumble make it to their kitchen with ingredient intact, it's time to make the dish.

programme.title: I Like To Ride My Bicycle (Mr. Monkey) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Let's go on a bike ride with Mr. Monkey and friends as we sing along to this Mr. Monkey version of the original Super Simple Song, "I Like To Ride My Bicycle." This is a great song to introduce during transportation themes! I like to ride my bicycle. I ride it every day. I ride it when I go to school. Or when I want to play.

programme.title: What's Your Name? - Kids Song - Finny the Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny the Shark is saying "Hello" and asking the question "What's your name?" This is a fun song to start the day at preschool! Hello, hello. What’s your name? Hello, hello. What’s your name? My name is Finny! My name is Myrtle! Nice to meet you.

programme.title: Wind The Bobbin Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Wind the bobbin up. Wind the bobbin up. Pull, pull, clap, clap, clap.” Get up and dance with this super fun version of the traditional English nursery rhyme.

programme.title: Hot Cross Buns - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s one of our favorite traditional nursery rhymes about food, made super simple. Come sing along with the Chef from our “Do You Know The Muffin Man?” and “Pat-A-Cake” videos. Yum yum!

programme.title: Flying Dragon Fruit Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Come forth brave warriors and watch the Bumble Nums catch some Flying Dragon Fruits for their fiery Flying Dragon Fruit Cake! But be careful! They breathe fire and are very fast!

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Dharma's Digger - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Oh no! While digging deep at a top secret construction site, Dhama’s digger gets splashed with smelly sludge! Everyone says that Carl's Car Wash is the place to get all cleaned up, so Dharma skedaddles over to see how Carl can help. With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Dharma’s vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Dharma rolls out one happy customer, and ready to get back to work on the site that Carl is itching to see!

programme.title: Mr. Golden Sun - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mister Sun, Sun, Mr. Golden Sun...please shine down on me! It’s the Mr. Sun song from Super Simple Songs!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A wedding spins into chaos as a giant ring escapes, but Big Wing’s catch saves the day and unveils a joyful bride. Later, Jett and Golden Boy use Cross Combo power to tame a hungry dino plant and rescue Golden Girl and Runi.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: On a Bermuda Triangle island, Jett and Runi battle a junk monster and restore the beach for Kyle and his dad. Then in Chiang Mai, the team douses flames, reunites baby elephant Paya with her mom, and celebrates by filming their happy day.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A cruise turns epic as Nako, with Super Wings and Traver’s time-freeze, saves the day from real pirates and earns his pirate crown. In New York, a wild AR game sends monsters loose until Joseph, Jett, Curie, and friends team up for a heroic roundup.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Yuto’s festival dream comes true as his new wand helps him shine and calm an enchanted dragon with the Super Wings. Later on Mars, the team helps Sam and Buddy collect rainbow stones, halt a volcano, and treasure a magical yellow gleam.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett brings a kite to a boy named Akas in the Himalayas, who wants to fly it in a kite festival. Red Pandas take off with the kite and Jett pursues them. When he runs into trouble and gets pinned under a rock, Dizzy shows up with her rescue gear to save the day.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett goes to Venice, Italy with a mask for Luca to wear at his masquerade party. Jett and Luca set out on a gondola to deliver the invitations, but when they run into a waterway traffic jam, Paul must show up to unclog the canal traffic.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Princess Maribelle from England asks for sparkly collars for her puppies to wear at her royal tea party. When the puppies escape and run every which way through the garden, Jett and Jerome join forces to round them up.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Riley from Hollywood is directing his first action film. Jett brings an alien mask and ends up acting as the monster. Riley’s demands get out of hand, so Jerome comes to co-star and help him bring about a peaceful ending.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett takes a pair of running shoes that light up to Lorna in Kenya. She’s going to run in a big race. While training she crosses paths with a lost baby wildebeast. Bello comes to play a game and reunite the baby with its parents.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett has a feathered headdress for Camila from Brazil. She’s dancing the samba in a Carnival parade. Jerome comes to help her with her dance moves and Jett uses his Jett Speed to fix Camila’s float when it suffers a breakdown.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Drew in Canada asks for ice skates so that he can play on an ice hockey team. When he gets to the rink he’s so nervous that he can’t play. Former Mountie Grand Albert gives Drew confidence-boosting tips so he can score the big goal.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Yuri from Russia is part of a family circus. He needs a unicycle for his act on the high wire. The rest of the family is on their way when the circus train breaks down. Jerome performs an amazing act for the crowd while Jett goes to help them.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Karl from Norway asks for a Viking helmet. He goes to Viking Camp where Jett is mistaken for a dragon. The campers chase Jett onto a ship that heads for a waterfall. Mira comes and pushes them away from disaster.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Martine in Paris, France needs a cake-decorating machine. Her father the baker asks her to ice a giant cake for a party at the Eiffel Tower. When the cake is ready the traffic is terrible. Dizzy flies the cake through the sky over the city.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Vietnamese sisters Sen and Kim are fans of pop star Kellie. They ask for wigs like the one their idol wears. When Kellie’s van gets stuck in a rice paddy, Dizzy comes to lead the way to the concert and the sisters are Kellie’s special guests.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett takes brushes to Willem in The Netherlands who is going to paint a mural on a sea wall. When the mural is almost done, a storm approaches. Jett and Donnie borrow the propeller from a windmill to dry the painting before it is ruined.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Cal's Sound Yard
programme.desc: Cal's Sound Yard is a new animated series from Baby Einstein that introduces the letters of the alphabet and the sounds they make. Why all the tracing and repetition? And why are the letters out of order? Don't worry! There's a reason for all that; the content is based on "The Einstein Way" - a curiosity-driven learning philosophy, from industry experts, inspired by Montessori and STEAM.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Join our playful puppet host, Bard the Dragon, on a fun-filled adventure using everyday words such as flower, cat, and moon, and beautiful scenery from nature. World Of Words lets you and your little one share the excitement of discovering new words together!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Every stage is an exciting adventure with your newborn. As babies grow, they absorb a wide variety of sights and sounds. And with so much to see and hear, infants seem to enjoy, and be most soothed by, gentle sounds such as your voice or pleasant, restful music. Treat your child -- and yourself -- to the delights of Baby Bach Musical Adventure, an award-winning program that exposes babies to the joy and majesty of classical music while presenting them with stimulating, colorful images.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover the wonderful world of water and deep dive into all the amazing creatures that call the ocean home. Learn simple words about the sea like water, coral reef, and ocean through narration and repetition. Engaging live-action footage of your favorite ocean friends and classical melodies will dazzle your little explorer as they experience the magical world under the sea.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Between 12 and 24 months, little ones become increasingly interested in more complex ideas and begin to explore numbers and counting. Numbers Nursery introduces little ones to numerals 1 through 5 in a captivating, interactive way that invites them to join in, clap their hands and count out loud.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Enjoy the best of summer, autumn and winter with Baby Einstein! Discover the familiar sights and sounds of the different seasons. Learn names of common objects associated with each through repetition and narration and see engaging images of whimsical toys that celebrate each. Discover the magic of each season from falling leaves, rolling beach balls and even snowmen.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet Cats, Dogs, Birds, Bugs and Woodland Creatures! Learn simple words through narration and repetition. Meet all sorts of creatures from domestic kittens to jungle cats, puppies, big and small birds, butterflies, lady bugs, otters and deer!! See whimsical toys and puppets.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids
programme.desc: Welcome to #Dinobees, a funny show for kids. It revolves around a family that discovers and adopts three dinosaurs. This comedy cartoon is perfect entertainment for toddlers.Dinobees has some amazing 3d animations. Get ready to have great jungle adventures with cartoon animals and dinosaurs.This entertaining playlist is a collection of videos for children like Fire Discovery, Fruits of Labour, Magical Berries, Catch me If You can, Glue Trap, Mis Splits, I Wanna Fly, The Gophers, Baby Sitter, Frozen Lake, Bubble Trouble, Where's My Water and many more videos.

programme.title: Transformex - Futuristic Cartoon Vehicles for Children
programme.desc: Welcome to the world of Trasnformex,a group of cartoon vehicles from the future that have the super hero abilty to transform themselves to any car or truck they like. programme.title: Super Supremes Wheels On The Bus & More Popular Kids Song

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik's momma escapes from the home for mothers of villains and demands her birthday present! When she endangers Sonic and Tails, and they manage to have her taken back to the home, even Robotnik is glad to see her go.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Coconuts is exiled by Robotnik to a remote jungle, where he uses a giant baby ape to destroy villages. But Sonic exposes Coconuts as a robot, and the baby ape's real Dad takes Coconuts and the baby ape away for a spanking.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: A fast talking, travelling salesman named Wes Weasley sells Robotnik various hedgehog-catching gadgets, including the Darkenator, the Super-duper Gravity Stopper, and the De-Atomizer.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic rescues Lucas, a rival of Robotnik's, from Robotnik's dungeons. But Robotnik will do anything to keep Lucas from finding his lost love, Lucinda, including proposing to her himself.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Big Griz, leader of a group of biker-bear rebels, mistakes Scratch and Grounder for Sonic and Tails, and teams up with S and G to attack the real Sonic and Tails.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Tails gets a megadose of Robotniks growth gas and grows quickly to monstrous proportions, eating everything in sight and accidentally terrorizing the countryside.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: When an undersea world is threatened by Captain Memo and his lobster sub, Sonic spreads a rumor that Memo plans to attack Robotnik next.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik pretends to retire, and lures Sonic and Tails into a phony vacation resort, laden with booby-traps set by Scratch and Grounder. But Sonic takes the bungee jumping, hang gliding, and skydiving in stride, and gives the bashes the bots.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic rescues Lucas, a rival of Robotnik's, from Robotnik's dungeons. But Robotnik will do anything to keep Lucas from finding his lost love, Lucinda, including proposing to her himself.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Tails gets trapped in the "Warp of Confusion" (an Escher-like world of another dimension) by Robotnik's cousin, Dr. Warpnik, Sonic comes to the rescue and traps Robotnik and his badniks in the warp.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik severely upsets the ecosystem above and below the surface, when he sends explosive, Robotnik snake probes drilling deep under ground in search of oil lakes.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Tails brings a new friend home to Knothole - a baby Terra-Pod who has strayed from the herd that is currently migrating. Sonic, Sally, and some of the other Freedom Fighters return the Baby-T to its mom and the rest of the herd.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters, with new addition, Dulcy, develop a De-Roboti-sizer and try it out on Bunnie. It's a success and, against Sally's wishes, Sonic battles Robotnik's newest weapon, a Virtual Reality Shriek-Bot.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters run into a Swashbuckler, named Ari, battling Swat-Bots. Sonic diverts them, allowing Ari to escape. After hearing that Robotnik is holding his band of Freedom Fighters hostage, Ari decides to help Sonic free them.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic attempts a solo rescue to prevent prisoners from being roboti-sized and is hit by a Memory Scrambler that erases short-term memory.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After a disastrous failed mission, Sonic laments that they can't go back and prevent Robotnik's take-over. Dulcy tells them they can and takes Sonic and Sally to the Floating Island where they find the "Time Stones".

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The friends are very tired because Mr. Moon hasn't come to sing them their nightly lullaby. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an Astronaut and flies to space to look for Mr. Moon with her friends. // In Rainbow Village, a super frantic Thunderbell is high up in the air, trapped inside a giant bubble. Thunderbell got trapped in a giant bubble in the middle of a bubble bath. In order to find out a way to bring Thunderbell back to earth, Rainbow Ruby turns into a scientist.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an architect and builds a house that suits Felicia's demands. // Gina is extremely worried. She needs strawberries for the Rainbow Fruit Ice Cream Festival that's starting soon but the strawberries are all mixed in with other red objects.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a farmer. // Thunderbell has a terrible tummy ache. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Microbiologist to find out why Thunderbell is sick. Rainbow Ruby uses a microscope to find a bacterium inside the cheesecake Thunderbell had but Felicia accidentally casts a spell that makes the bacterium gigantic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling takes Rainbow Ruby to Ellie's cabin where she meets mischievous baby stars that fell from the sky. The baby stars were playing together, got separated from their mother and ended up in Rainbow Village. // Kiki is extremely upset because the jewel Prince Frederick gave her as a gift broke. The friends try to glue the broken pieces together but it doesn't work. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a jewelry designer and tries designing jewelry with the broken pieces.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ruby is looking at shoes that are now too small for her but she is unwilling to throw away. Ling Ling is worried about Thunderbell. His scooter broke so he is not able to carry out his work as a mail carrier. // Gina calls Rainbow Ruby because today is the day to show their appreciation to Mayor Ling Ling. It took a long time for the village friends to prepare an amazing painting for the mayor. Unfortunately, Thunderbell accidentally destroyed the painting while delivering it.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: There is a new menu at the restaurant, which is very popular. There is a long line in front of the restaurant but there weren't enough staff members to serve the villagers the food. Rainbow Ruby turns into a waitress using her magic suitcase and serve tables kindly. // Rainbow Village is full of marbles. The marbles that Rainbow Ruby spilled in her room poured down into the village from up above the sky! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a paramedic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: In Rainbow Village, people are busy preparing for a dance marathon, a competition in which all of the villagers participate. Ling Ling is very sad that Ellie decided not to participate. Ellie is not a confident dancer so she doesn't want to participate. // Rainbow Ruby's friends tell her that they are now friends with Felicia. They were playing hide and seek but Felicia is such a good hider that she's refused to come out even though she's won the game.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ellie's and Jessy's hair are a complete mess after Felicia accidentally causes some Rainbow fruit to fall on them. programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Melanie is sad because she misses her friend, Marisa who is visiting relatives in India. Barney takes her and the other kids on a trip to the magic caboose to visit pretend countries like Egypt, Tahiti, and India. Barney and the little dinosaurs enjoy an Art Festival. BJ and Riff share some musical art and Baby Bop shares a painting of a very special friendBarney!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When some of the children invite Barney to join them for a race, BJ and Baby Bop decide hell need their help. By the end of the day, everyone finds out that there are many fun ways to exercise. Baby Bop cant wait for BJ and Riff to go on their camping trip so she can have Barney and the park all to herself! But, once theyre gone, she discovers that as more time that goes by, the more she misses them!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney is snacking on pistachios...and just can't seem to stop! His friends try to help. An unexpected avalanche of pistachios convinces Barney that his friends may be right. BJ has a dream after eating too many sweets, where he eats as many sweets as he wants and ends up with a big stomachache. When he wakes up, hes happy to find out it was only a dream. When he asks for a healthy snack, his friends are happy to oblige.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When Baby Bop becomes a little bossy playing with her friends, Barney teaches her that being bossy isn't a nice way to treat your friends. Riff feels terrible when he accidentally wrecks BJs beloved scooter. BJ loved that scooter more than almost anything! Riff and Baby Bop decide to have a sale in the park to raise money to buy BJ a brand-new scooter.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is having a hard time finding a story she hasn't heard before. Barney and the dinos decide to tell her an old story with a new twist...by singing Little Red Riding Hood! When a wheelchair-bound child is unintentionally neglected by his friends, Barney steps in to help. He reminds the others to include the child by showing them that even Barney feels excluded sometimes. In the end, everyone is included for a day of fun!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is sad because she lost her pet caterpillar, Monty. Barney and the others try to help her find it. Baby Bop is surprised to discover Monty inside a chrysalis. Everyone is excited to find Monty now a beautiful butterfly! Rachel carelessly litters in the park then later has a dream about animals who talk. With Barney and the animals help, Rachel learns that the park is home to many. Rachel realizes the importance of nature and of keeping our earth clean!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ receives a letter from his pen pal in China revealing that his pen pal will be coming to visit. With the help of friends, BJ hurries to get the park ready. He wants it decorated so his pen pal feels right at home. BJ soon learns that his pen pal wants to see how BJ really lives. Barney has a tee-rific surprise for BJ and Baby Bop. Cousin Riff is moving to town!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ is pretending to be a great detective and a case quickly comes his way when something nibbles on one of Baby Bops healthy snacks. BJ, Barney, and Baby Bop uncover several clues along the way and soon realize the culprit is a pet rabbit. When Riff hears a mysterious sound in the park, he plays detective. Along the way, they explore the sense of sound while identifying sounds of nature and the neighborhood.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop wants everyone to play with her instead of working in their garden. They promise to play with her as soon as they finish. Baby Bop can't wait, and tries many ways to get them to come play with her now. When her friends do come to play, they have a tea party and enjoy some of the fresh things from the garden. Riff invents a Happiness Machine to try to cheer up a sad little girl. But it's her friends, not the machine, that make her happy!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Everyone loves Barney! When Ryan tries to be just like Barney, he has a hard time filling Barney's shoes. Ryan soon learns that he is special when he's just being himself! Barney's friends are having a hard time deciding what to do because everyone wants to do the same thing! Barney's Native American tale, The Chase, shows them that it's best to think for themselves and not follow the crowd.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and Riff help Baby Bop learn how to follow the rules and learn that rules make games fun for everyone! Baby Bop is having one of those days! Every time she turns around, someone is telling her no! As her frustration grows, Barney explains that the word no can help you avoid making mistakes or getting hurt.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: There's a fishing contest in the park! When Ryan is tempted not to follow the rules, Barney tells The Emperor's Contest and Ryan discovers that honesty is the best policy. A talented young artist is painting a portrait of Barney, but when she steps away, BJ takes credit for it. When the others beg him to teach them to paint, BJ admits the truth and learns that honesty is the best policy!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: An ordinary game of hide and seek takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! BJ thinks that his sister may be seeing things but Barney takes this opportunity to help the young dinos learn about their sense of sight. When Riff meets a friend who can't hear well, Riff wonders if he would still like music. Barney tells everyone about Beethoven, a famous musician who wrote beautiful music even though he couldn't hear it.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: A mysterious paper airplane flies into the park, inspiring the children to talk about all things Airplanes! The children let their creativity fly when they use their imaginations to build their very own airplane.

programme.title: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Landslide: A Portrait of President Herbert Hoover
programme.desc: Herbert Hoover was a politician and a humanitarian whose legacy has been largely defined by the Great Depression. When he was elected president in a landslide victory in 1928, Hoover had never before held public office. Even so, his humanitarian work and career as a mining engineer, businessman and U.S. Secretary of Commerce carried him to a win. When the stock market crashed on October 29, 1929, Hoovers agenda was derailed by the worsening economic crisis. Despite various efforts to pull the nation out of depression, Hoovers unsuccessful attempts led to his sound defeat by Franklin Roosevelt just four years after his landslide victory. LANDSLIDE looks at the life of the controversial figure through interviews with notable historians and Hoover scholars.The program explores the facts and fictions behind the presidency of Herbert Hoover including The Great Depression and the role of the Federal Reserve. Hoovers early life in Iowa and abroad, the international experiences that led to his decision to run for office, his presidency and political philosophy, and the lasting impacts of his policy decisions during and after the depression are revealed. Through interviews with notable scholars such as David Kennedy, Amity Shlaes, Robert Reich, Tim Egan and Timothy Walch, this biography sheds new light on Hoovers difficult tenure and enduring legacy.

programme.title: Uncle Jack: The Manhattan Project and Beyond
programme.desc: The Manhattan Project was an enormous undertaking that required the efforts of many of the world's most brilliant intellectuals. Hundreds of physicists, mathematicians, and engineers were needed to design, build, and test the world's first atomic weapon and the Unites States government did everything in its power to lure these individuals to the Manhattan Project. Documentary includes: Interviews with Scientists conducted by the World War II Foundation Interviews with World War II Historians, Interviews with WWII veterans Interviews with those who worked with John Gray in the world of Atomic Energy, Interviews with authors who have written extensively about the Manhattan Project, Interviews with people from the world of academia. This film is personal: One of those assigned to the project was my uncle John Edmund Gray, a University of Rhode Island graduate with a brilliant mind.Tim Gray

programme.title: Paleo Sleuths
programme.desc: Enter a prehistoric world when mammals ruled supreme and embark on a fossil safari with paleontologists (paleo sleuths) that reveals how changing climate transformed North America and the exotic mammals that lived there from 34 million years ago. This stunning documentary explores excavation sites across Americas heartland, including the incredible Ashfall Fossil Beds -- a graveyard of ancient rhinos, horses, camels, birds and carnivorous dogs. Lying in their death poses, the animals died of asphyxiation from falling ash from a 12 million year old super volcano. In another fascinating segment, two complete skeletons of bull mammoths are discovered locked together by their tusks and paleo sleuths investigate clues that might tell the story of their tragic death.With drone footage, 3-D models, cutting-edge animations, imaginative time-lapse sequences and paleontoligists in the field, PALEO SLEUTHS brings the age of mammals to life.

programme.title: Survivors of Malmedy: December 1944
programme.desc: SURVIVORS OF MALMEDY: DECEMBER 1944 tells the story of the World War II massacre of 84 American soldiers in Belgium during the opening days of the famous Battle of the Bulge.On December 17, 1944, after a short battle, more than 130 American GIs were taken prisoner just outside the Belgian town of Malmedy and herded into a field by an infamous German SS division led by one of the Nazi's most brutal commanders. The unarmed American prisoners were gunned down. Those still alive after the initial shooting in the brutal cold were killed by SS soldiers ordered into the field to execute any Americans still breathing. Some American soldiers played dead. Some ran. Some escaped to nearby houses. In the end, more than 50 Americans did escape the infamous Malmedy Massacre. Today, just a handful of soldiers remain.Weaving interviews with the few remaining survivors of the massacre with archival footage, and drone video shot in the Malmedy area and in the field where the infamous event occurred, the emotional and powerful SURVIVORS OF MALMEDY chronicles what became known as the largest single mass murder of American troops in World War II.

programme.title: Unsettled History: America, China, and the Doolittle Tokyo Raid
programme.desc: The Doolittle Raiders were one of the most famous American aviation units of World War II. The new documentary UNSETTLED HISTORY: AMERICA, CHINA AND THE DOOLITTLE TOKYO RAID examines a key moment in American/Chinese history, exploring how the two sides recall a daring 1942 raid on Tokyo, and how this shared event is remembered in different ways, the reasons for this divergence and what lessons it may hold for today. In the film, the memory of the Raiders is kept alive by their children and grand-children, as well as by descendants of the courageous Chinese men and women who rescued many of them from likely capture, torture, or even death at the hands of the Japanese military. On April 18, 1942, sixteen U.S. Army aircrews embarked on their first combat mission. They had enough fuel to reach their targets -- military and industrial sites in Tokyo and five other Japanese cities but not enough to go much farther. Even if they somehow made their way to the relative safety of China, no one knew they were coming. In 2018, the children of the Doolittle Raiders visited China, to literally walk in their fathers footsteps. But is this mission remembered in China as heroic or misguided? And what kind of reception did they receive at a time when US/China relations were bad, and getting worse? One historical event, two different narratives.

programme.title: Fannie's Last Supper
programme.desc: Join culinary expert Christopher Kimball and his team of test kitchen sleuths as they attempt to recreate an authentic turn-of-the-century 12-course dinner. For two years, Kimball painstakingly researched and tested recipes from The Boston Cooking School Cook Book, written in 1896 by Fannie Farmer. Tracking down unusual ingredients, discovering forgotten techniques and battling a finicky wood stove are just part of the fun as the pressure builds for the crew to pull off a perfectly-orchestrated meal for their distinguished guests.The project required the team, lead by head chef Erin McMurrin, to constantly regulate the heat on a cast-iron wood stove and work in the Victorian-era kitchen without the benefit of modern appliances. The courses they prepare include: rissoles (filled and fried puff pastry), mock turtle soup with fried brain balls, lobster lAmricaine, roast goose with chestnut stuffing and jus, wood-grilled salmon, roast saddle of venison, Canton punch, three molded Victorian jellies and a spectacular French-inspired Mandarin cake.This special details not only the cooking and serving of the dinner itself, but travels back in time to investigate the life of Fannie Farmer and the world of Victorian cooking, including the foods, the cookware, and the kitchens in an effort to connect the present to the past.

programme.title: We Hold These Truths: The Global Quest for Liberty
programme.desc: This lively documentary explores the promise and global influence of Americas expression of freedom, the Declaration of Independence. Since 1776, more than 100 nations have declared their independence, modeling their declarations on Americas. From Vietnam to Venezuela, from Prague to Hong Kong, millions of lives have been touched by the ideals written in this enduring document. Host Douglas Ginsburg, Federal Judge on the U.S. Court of Appeals, D.C. Circuit, interviews leading experts and ordinary citizens from around the world. In Paris, at the Palace of Versailles, he meets a descendant of the Marquis de Lafayette. In Prague, he tours a cold war bunker and learns that the Czech Republic's journey toward independence mirrors the three great waves of independence that swept the world in the twentieth century. He meets immigrants from Hong Kong, Venezuela, and Vietnam, all seeking the ideals of Life, Liberty and the Pursuit of Happiness. In the United States, Judge Ginsburg travels from Philadelphia to Baltimore to New York and beyond to learn how the words of the Declaration influenced the abolitionist movement, the Women's Suffrage movement, and iconic civil rights figures like Frederick Douglass and Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. WE HOLD THESE TRUTHS is a revealing and fascinating look at this extraordinary and enduring document.
